title: Mizuho OSI Trios Surgical Table System - Table Top Not Communicating with Base
description: Identifies causes of table top communication failure with base, including cabling, connector, power, and control interface issues before assuming internal electronics failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the table is not supporting a patient. If in use, safely transfer the patient to another table before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect Power to Table Top and Base
steps.instructions: Verify both table top and base are powered. Check power cords, plugs, and outlet functionality. No power can prevent communication.

steps.title: Check Connection Cables
steps.instructions: Inspect the communication cable between table top and base for secure attachment, damage, or corrosion. Reseat or replace cable if necessary.

steps.title: Verify Connector Orientation
steps.instructions: Ensure connectors are fully seated in the correct orientation. Misaligned connectors can prevent proper data exchange.

steps.title: Check Indicator Lights
steps.instructions: Observe status LEDs on the table top and base. Blinking or off lights may indicate connection, power, or fault issues.

steps.title: Test Hand Control/Interface Panel
steps.instructions: Operate table functions via hand control or interface panel. Lack of response may reinforce a communication failure.

steps.title: Swap with Known Good Table Top (if available)
steps.instructions: If possible, connect a verified working table top to the base. Communication restores function indicates the issue is with the original table top.

CCR.Complaint: Table top not responding or communicating with base.
CCR.Cause: Communication cable was loose, preventing data exchange between table top and base.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated cable and verified proper connection; table functions restored.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: operational after cable reseating

title: Mizuho OSI Trios Surgical Table System - Movement Functions Inoperative or Limited Range
description: Identifies causes of inoperative or limited table movement, including power, control, connection, actuator, and safety lock issues before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the table is not supporting a patient. If in use, safely transfer the patient to another table before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Power Supply
steps.instructions: Check that the table is plugged into a live, tested outlet. Inspect power cords for damage or loose connections. Movement will not function without proper power.

steps.title: Check Battery or Backup Power
steps.instructions: If the table has a battery, ensure it is charged and properly seated. Limited movement can occur if battery voltage is low.

steps.title: Inspect Hand Control and Pendant
steps.instructions: Confirm the hand control or pendant is properly connected and undamaged. Test buttons or switches for responsiveness. A faulty control can prevent table movement.

steps.title: Examine Connection Between Base and Table Top
steps.instructions: Ensure all connectors between the base and the table top are fully seated. Loose or damaged connections can inhibit movement.

steps.title: Check Safety Locks or Brakes
steps.instructions: Verify that all mechanical locks, brakes, or actuators are disengaged. Engaged locks may restrict or prevent movement.

steps.title: Test Movement Functions Individually
steps.instructions: Attempt to operate each movement function (height, tilt, lateral, Trendelenburg) separately. Note any movement, unusual resistance, sounds, or partial function.

steps.title: Inspect for Obstructions
steps.instructions: Look under and around the table for any items or cables obstructing movement. Even small obstructions can limit range or prevent actuation.

CCR.Complaint: Surgical table movement functions inoperative or limited.
CCR.Cause: Hand control and base-to-top connections verified; mechanical locks engaged and obstructing travel.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared obstructions and disengaged locks; functions restored and table tested.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual resistance or sounds during testing
Final device status (operational, limited, or out of service)

title: Mizuho OSI Trios Surgical Table System - Power or Battery Failure
description: Identifies causes of power or battery failure, including power source, cord, battery, and connection issues before assuming internal electronics failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the table is not supporting a patient. If in use, safely transfer the patient to another table before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify External Power Source
steps.instructions:
Check that the outlet is live using a known working device.
Inspect the power cord for damage or loose connections.
Plug the table directly into a verified outlet (avoid extension cords).
Expected Outcome: Table powers on or battery begins charging.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Status
steps.instructions:
Locate the table’s battery pack(s).
Check for loose connections or visible damage.
Confirm battery charge indicator (if available).
Expected Outcome: Battery shows charge or begins charging when connected to power.

steps.title: Swap or Recharge Battery
steps.instructions:
If a spare or replacement battery is available, install it to verify the issue.
Ensure charging station or built-in charger is functioning.
Expected Outcome: Table powers on with a known good battery.

steps.title: Check Control Panel/Hand Pendant Response
steps.instructions:
Attempt to power on using the main control panel and hand pendant.
Observe any LED indicators, error codes, or alarm beeps.
Expected Outcome: Table responds to commands, or error code indicates battery/power fault.

steps.title: Inspect Fuses and Circuit Breakers
steps.instructions:
If accessible, check table fuses and any onboard circuit breakers.
Replace blown fuses with manufacturer-specified types only.
Expected Outcome: Table powers on or fault clears.

CCR.Complaint: Table will not power on; battery does not charge.
CCR.Cause: Battery pack faulty or external power source interrupted.
CCR.Resolution: Tested outlet and power cord; swapped battery with known good pack; table powers on.

title: Mizuho OSI Trios Surgical Table System - Locking Mechanism Not Engaging
description: Identifies causes of the table’s locking mechanism failing to engage, including power, control, actuator, or safety interlock issues before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the table is not supporting a patient. Do not attempt locking mechanism troubleshooting while a patient is on the table.

steps.title: Visual Inspection of Locking Mechanism
steps.instructions: Examine the lock lever, pins, or mechanical parts for obstruction, debris, or visible damage. Clear any debris and ensure proper alignment. Expected outcome: unobstructed, smooth movement.

steps.title: Check Table Power
steps.instructions: Verify the table is powered on and battery or AC supply is functional. Locking actuators may require electrical power. Expected outcome: table powers normally.

steps.title: Inspect Control Panel / Handset Commands
steps.instructions: Attempt engaging the lock from the hand control or pendant. Observe indicator lights or audible clicks confirming activation. Expected outcome: lock engages or indicator responds.

steps.title: Confirm Safety Interlocks
steps.instructions: Ensure all safety interlocks are properly seated (e.g., brakes, caster locks, accessories). Many tables will not engage the lock if interlocks detect unsafe conditions. Expected outcome: lock engages when all interlocks satisfied.

steps.title: Test Actuator Function
steps.instructions: If safe, listen for actuator movement when lock command is given. No movement may indicate actuator or wiring issue. Expected outcome: audible actuator operation.

steps.title: Swap or Inspect Cables
steps.instructions: Check connections between control panel and lock actuator for loose or damaged cables. Swap with known-good cables if available. Expected outcome: lock functions normally with proper connection.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported the table locking mechanism would not engage.
CCR.Cause: Safety interlocks were functional, actuator did not respond to lock command.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service and sent for repair; Out of Service label applied.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: Out of Service

title: Mizuho OSI Trios Surgical Table System - Accessory Interface or Attachment Recognition Issues
description: Identifies causes of accessory or attachment recognition failure, including connection, interface, power, or software issues before assuming internal electronics failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the table is not supporting a patient. No troubleshooting should occur while a patient is on the table.

steps.title: Inspect Physical Connections
steps.instructions: Check that all accessories and attachments are properly seated and locked in their ports. Loose or misaligned connectors often prevent recognition.

steps.title: Check Interface Cables
steps.instructions: Inspect cables connecting the table to accessory interfaces for damage, wear, or disconnection. Replace or reseat as needed.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Table
steps.instructions: Turn the table off, wait 30 seconds, and power it back on. Some recognition issues clear after a reset.

steps.title: Verify Accessory Compatibility
steps.instructions: Confirm that the attached accessory is compatible with the Trios system model. Non-compatible or defective attachments may not be recognized.

steps.title: Observe Indicator Lights or Error Messages
steps.instructions: Look for blinking lights, LEDs, or display notifications on the table and accessory. They can indicate specific connection or interface faults.

steps.title: Swap Accessories or Ports
steps.instructions: If possible, try another known-working accessory in the same interface, or the same accessory in another compatible port, to isolate the issue.

CCR.Complaint: Table does not recognize attached accessory during procedure.
CCR.Cause: Accessory port or interface cable loose, or accessory incompatible.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated connections, verified compatible accessory; issue persisted, sent for service evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Indicator lights or error codes observed
